## Overview

SmartSimple Cloud for Granting is a configurable cloud-based grant-management, research-funding and CSR platform developed by SmartSimple Software (Toronto, Canada). A UK entity, SmartSimple Software Ltd, supplies the UK market and is listed on G-Cloud 13 / 14. In 2024 SmartSimple completed a merger with Foundant Technologies (US nonprofit tech). The platform is used internationally by government agencies, research funders and corporate foundations.

## Products & Services

- **SmartSimple Cloud for Granting** — end-to-end grant lifecycle: scheme definition, applications, eligibility, assessment, contracts, payments, monitoring.
- **Configurable data model** down to custom entities, fields and workflows; strong for complex programmes.
- **SmartSimple Cloud platform** — same substrate used for research, CSR and reviews.

## Pricing Model

- Not publicly disclosed on G-Cloud 14 pricing document.
- Industry benchmark: £50k–£200k+ Year 1 for a UK public-sector deployment, depending on user numbers, modules and configuration complexity.
- Typical contract: annual SaaS subscription + implementation (often co-delivered with a partner).

## UK Government Presence

- **G-Cloud 14 listed**: yes — [SmartSimple Cloud for Granting on Digital Marketplace](https://www.applytosupply.digitalmarketplace.service.gov.uk/g-cloud/services/381817298686710)
- **DOS listed**: not specifically.
- **UK data centres**: yes (UK hosting available per vendor documentation).
- Known for complex grant programmes, including some UK public-sector funders; specific contract names not publicly disclosed.

## Strengths

- Deepest configurability among the commercial shortlist — the data model, workflow engine, portals and reporting are all end-user configurable.
- Used for complex, multi-stage funding programmes with contracts and payments.
- Strong API surface for integration.

## Weaknesses

- Requires significant configuration effort; "time to go live" typically 3–6 months minimum.
- UK-specific guardrails (Green Book, TAG) are not native.
- Heavier cost base than Flexi-Grant for a starting configuration.
- Not GOV.UK Design System native; UX re-theming required.
- Canadian parent / US merger — watch for product roadmap uncertainty.

## Projects Referenced In

- DfT Transforming City Regions Funding (discovery, 2026-04-20)
